title: Podman nginx container not awnsering pfsense port forwarding
body:
Hi! im trying to get the following setup working with podman:

`Wan physical interface` --> `PFsense` --> `Lan interface (br0)` --> `nginx podman container (10.0.0.10)`

Everything is working locally and I can indeed curl the container from both a VM (on br0) and using PFsense shell, but when I try to portforward to nginx container it does not seem to reach it.

How I created the container:
```
podman create --net nginx-net --expose 80 -p 0.0.0.0::80 --name nginx docker.io/library/nginx:latest
```

the net its connected to:
```
[root@leierpc leier]# cat /etc/cni/net.d/nginx-net.conflist 
{
   "cniVersion": "0.4.0",
   "name": "nginx-net",
   "plugins": [
      {
         "type": "bridge",
         "bridge": "br0",
         "ipam": {
            "type": "static",
	   "addresses": [
		{
			"address": "10.0.0.10/24",
			"gateway": "10.0.0.1"
		}
	   ]
         },
	"routes": {
		"dst": "0.0.0.0/0", "gw": "10.0.0.1"
	}
      }
  ]
}
```
tcpdump:

```
[root@leierpc leier]# tcpdump -n -i br0
dropped privs to tcpdump
tcpdump: verbose output suppressed, use -v or -vv for full protocol decode
listening on br0, link-type EN10MB (Ethernet), capture size 262144 bytes
19:24:00.536613 IP 88.88.63.41.41642 > 10.0.0.10.http: Flags [S], seq 1869193404, win 64240, options [mss 1460,sackOK,TS val 110282405 ecr 0,nop,wscale 7], length 0
19:24:00.786693 IP 88.88.63.41.41644 > 10.0.0.10.http: Flags [S], seq 421737515, win 64240, options [mss 1460,sackOK,TS val 110282655 ecr 0,nop,wscale 7], length 0
19:24:01.560618 IP 88.88.63.41.41642 > 10.0.0.10.http: Flags [S], seq 1869193404, win 64240, options [mss 1460,sackOK,TS val 110283429 ecr 0,nop,wscale 7], length 0
19:24:01.800553 IP 88.88.63.41.41644 > 10.0.0.10.http: Flags [S], seq 421737515, win 64240, options [mss 1460,sackOK,TS val 110283669 ecr 0,nop,wscale 7], length 0
19:24:03.670539 IP 88.88.63.41.41642 > 10.0.0.10.http: Flags [S], seq 1869193404, win 64240, options [mss 1460,sackOK,TS val 110285539 ecr 0,nop,wscale 7], length 0
19:24:03.880442 IP 88.88.63.41.41644 > 10.0.0.10.http: Flags [S], seq 421737515, win 64240, options [mss 1460,sackOK,TS val 110285749 ecr 0,nop,wscale 7], length 0
```
if you need any more info please feel free to ask.
please help, I really would like to use podman instead of docker! : ) 

for more help, @ some helpfull people
@dcbw @bboreham @mccv1r0

comments.body:
I have tried it before, and I tried it again now. Same result just no answer from the container. 

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

also something worth mentioning is that in the nginx container logs, the port forward attempts are not logged, only the curls that goes successful.

@leier69: Based on the info you supplied above, your bridge br0 doesn't have an IP address or subnet AFAICT.  The container on that bridge will have the static address 10.0.0.10 with prefix of 24.  

The only reason to use ```-p``` is to connect to an IP address on the host and have the host (e.g. ```iptables```) forward that port into the container.  Your ```tcpdump``` reads like you are trying to ```curl``` to 10.0.0.10 from the host (or possibly a remote node) with source IP of  88.88.63.41.  I'm guessing that the host has no idea who to route the packet.

Port 80 is in use for me, so I tried with tcp/5222.  When I use ```--expose 5222 -p 5222:5222```  (syntax I'm familiar with) and put 10.0.0.1/24 on br0 (via ``` ip addr add 10.0.0.1/24 dev br0```) I'm able to 

1/ connect to 10.0.0.1 port 5222 with your setup
2/ connect to 10.0.0.10 port 5222 directly (no need to do port forwarding at all)

Note: you might need to set ```iptables``` FORWARD chain default policy to ACCEPT
10.0.0.0 is the network address for the 10.0.0.0/24 subnet; you can use 10.0.0.1 - 10.0.0.254 (.255 is broadcast address.)
